title: Never Miss a Market Beat: Realtime Stock Tickers in Your Terminal with Rust!
contentMarkdown:
## 📝 Quick Summary: 
Tickrs is a Rust-based command-line application that provides real-time stock ticker data directly in the terminal. It leverages Yahoo! Finance for data and offers various customization options for displaying stock information, charts, and market hours.

## 🔑 Key Takeaways

- ✅ Monitor real-time stock and crypto data directly in your terminal.

- ✅ Built with Rust for high performance, efficiency, and reliability.

- ✅ Offers customizable chart types (line, candle, kagi) and timeframes.

- ✅ Eliminates context switching by providing market insights within your development environment.

- ✅ A great example of a sophisticated Text User Interface (TUI) application for Rust developers.


## 📊 Project Statistics
- ⭐ Stars: 1612
- 🍴 Forks: 92
- ❗ Open Issues: 27


## 🛠 Tech Stack
- ✅ Rust


Are you a developer who likes to keep an eye on the market but hates context switching to a browser or a heavy app? What if you could have real-time stock data, beautifully displayed, right there in your terminal? Enter `tick-rs`, a fantastic open-source project that brings your stock market watchlist directly into your command line interface. It's designed for efficiency and convenience, allowing you to stay informed without ever leaving your development environment.Imagine a sleek, interactive dashboard showing your favorite stock tickers updating live. That's exactly what `tick-rs` delivers. Built with the performance and reliability of Rust, this tool pulls real-time data from Yahoo! Finance and renders it as a stunning text-based user interface (TUI). You can visualize market movements with different chart types – choose from classic line charts, detailed candlestick charts (perfect for technical analysis), or even Kagi charts. The interface is highly interactive, letting you switch between symbols, adjust timeframes (from 1D to 5Y), and view different chart styles on the fly. It's incredibly responsive and feels just like a native GUI application, but it lives entirely within your terminal.Setting up `tick-rs` is a breeze, whether you prefer installing via Cargo, Homebrew, or downloading a pre-compiled binary. Once installed, you can launch it with a simple command, specifying your desired ticker symbols. The project also allows for extensive customization through command-line flags or a configuration file. Want to see pre/post-market data? No problem. Need to adjust the data update interval? You got it. You can even choose to show volume graphs or hide previous close lines to tailor the display precisely to your needs. This level of control ensures that `tick-rs` fits seamlessly into your workflow.For developers, `tick-rs` is more than just a cool gadget; it's a productivity enhancer. It eliminates the need to constantly open resource-heavy browser tabs or dedicated trading applications just to glance at a stock price. This means less context switching, fewer distractions, and a more streamlined way to monitor market trends while you're coding. Its lightweight nature, powered by Rust, ensures minimal system impact. Furthermore, for those interested in TUI development, `tick-rs` serves as an excellent example of a well-crafted, performant terminal application written in Rust, offering insights into building complex interactive interfaces without a GUI framework. It truly offers a unique blend of utility, performance, and developer-friendly design.

[View the Project on GitHub](https://github.com/tarkah/tickrs)
